Phase-30.NAME: KPprotoN Runtime Bridge
Phase-30.Goal: Connect KrotPN MTProto assignments to a runtime policy bridge that can activate issued SNI domains safely.

NonGoals.item-2: No user dashboard UI work yet.

Steps.step-1: Define the bridge interface for apply-domain-policy, runtime health, startup replay, and reconciliation.
Steps.step-2: Choose integration shape for KPprotoN/mtproto_proxy runtime with pinned dependency or isolated service boundary.
Steps.step-3: Implement startup replay from M-042 assignments into runtime policy state with idempotent retries.
Steps.step-4: Implement degraded-mode semantics when the runtime bridge is unavailable during verified onboarding.
Steps.step-5: Wire a scheduler reconciliation task without impacting existing anti-abuse scheduler jobs.
Steps.step-6: Add tests for policy apply success, bridge unavailable, replay after restart, and no-secret logging.

Verification.command: cd backend && SECRET_KEY=test-secret-key-with-enough-length DATA_ENCRYPTION_KEY=dpFmfSVqfdAK3yx5MQV6Tcv35RZuw5MzaH0yhxJ01q0= PYTHONPATH=. ../.venv/bin/python -m pytest -o addopts='' tests/test_kpproton_runtime_bridge.py tests/test_mtproto_assignment_registry.py tests/test_mtproto_provisioning.py tests/test_scheduler.py -v
python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-30.xml docs/verification/V-M-044.xml docs/verification-index.xml; mygrace lint

VerificationMatrix.matrix-1: Fake-adapter tests for apply-domain-policy, runtime health, startup replay, reconciliation, degraded mode, and secret redaction.
VerificationMatrix.matrix-2: Assignment registry and owner-safe provisioning tests remain green so bridge policy state follows Postgres assignment truth.
VerificationMatrix.matrix-3: Scheduler tests prove MTProto policy sync is registered once and does not regress existing recurring jobs.
VerificationMatrix.matrix-4: No deploy/install script changes are allowed in Phase-30; shared 443 edge and TLS/domain work are deferred to Phase-32.

PreflightGates.gate-1: Execution uses fake/local KPprotoN adapters only; no live Telegram, DNS, TLS, host networking, or shared 443 edge mutation.
PreflightGates.gate-2: Deploy/install scripts and nginx/docker-compose edge surfaces remain unchanged unless the operator explicitly opens Phase-32 work.
PreflightGates.gate-3: Bridge outage must produce explicit degraded MTProto state and must not block normal KrotPN login, verified onboarding, billing, or VPN config access.
PreflightGates.gate-4: Runtime logs, health payloads, and admin summaries must redact tg://proxy links, derived secrets, base secret, salt, tokens, and adapter credentials.

AcceptanceCriteria.item-1: Active assignments replay into policy state after backend or bridge restart.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-2: Bridge failure does not break normal KrotPN login or VPN config access.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-3: Verified users see an explicit pending/degraded proxy state if runtime activation is delayed.
AcceptanceCriteria.item-4: Runtime logs include stable markers but no full proxy link, token, or secret.

Completion.item-1: M-044 now exposes a secret-free runtime bridge boundary with apply-domain-policy, runtime health, startup replay, and scheduler reconciliation entry points.
Completion.item-2: The Phase-30 integration shape is an isolated backend service boundary using fake/local adapters only; live edge, DNS, TLS, and shared 443 work remain deferred.
Completion.item-3: Phase-30 backend verification passed locally: 21 targeted tests across runtime bridge, assignment registry, provisioning, and scheduler coverage.

Risks.risk-1: KPprotoN Erlang dependency drift can make runtime behavior non-reproducible unless pinned.
Risks.risk-2: Policy state can drift from Postgres unless reconciliation is designed early.
